metadata.scope: Baity branch integration candidates only; no entities.json changes

confidence_scale.HIGH: Directly supported by a cited record page or archival item description
confidence_scale.MEDIUM: Strong but at least one step is derivative or inferential
confidence_scale.LOW: Useful lead, not safe as a proved tree fact

guardrails: Do not claim an immigrant arrival for the Baity line from this file
Do not treat the Reeves County April 1901 purchase as proved until the land instrument is cited
Do not treat George Baity/Batee/Baty in 1774 as a proved direct-line node from this file alone

tree_ready_candidates.statement: W. A. Baity and Paralee Baity were a married couple active in Bexar County real estate by 1910.
notes: Newspaper transfer and archival deed description independently name the couple

tree_ready_candidates.statement: W. A. Baity was a San Antonio builder/developer by 1909 and remained publicly associated with homebuilding in the 1920s.
safe_tree_use: occupation note: builder / developer in San Antonio

tree_ready_candidates.statement: W. A. Baity was listed at 211 Castillo Ave., San Antonio, by July 2, 1925.
Isham "Isom" Baity is listed at Courtney Baptist Church Cemetery with dates 19 Oct 1804 to 23 Dec 1892.
Jennette C. Baity Hutchens is explicitly identified as a daughter of Isham Baity and Nancy Plowman.
safe_tree_use: supporting evidence for the Isham Baity and Nancy Plowman family unit

tree_ready_candidates.statement: The 1830 Surry County census transcription lists David Baity, George Baity, Isam Baity, and Pleasant Baity as separate heads of household.
notes: This supports Baity presence in Surry County by 1830, not a parent-child chain

research_note_only.statement: The family's Pearl Baity is probably the same woman as record-name Paralee Baity, but this pass did not recover a direct alias record.
Earlier repo work identifies W. A. as William Alexander Baity, but this pass did not newly re-cite a public marriage or death record proving the expansion.
A derivative death-index page gives Paralee Johnson Baity as dying on 12-26-1969 in Bexar County, widowed.
notes: Use only after direct Texas death record confirmation

research_note_only.statement: Family oral history says Pearl Baity bought Reeves County land in April 1901 and retained the mineral rights, but this pass did not recover the deed or patent.
notes: Geology context is real; the land event is still unproved here

research_note_only.statement: A shared compiled note places George Beate/Batee/Baty in Rowan County records by May 6, 1774, with later 1785, 1787, 1798, and 1800 references.
notes: Treat as a lead to original Rowan/Surry court, deed, tax, and census images
